detaileddescription.para: CRC4 polynomial
These values should be used with the CRC dispatch function.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t poly, uint16_t seed, const uint8_t *src, size_t len)
memberdef.name: crc16

briefdescription.para: Generic function for computing a CRC-16 without input or output reflection.

detaileddescription.para: Compute CRC-16 by passing in the address of the input, the input length and polynomial used in addition to the initial value. This is O(n*8) where n is the length of the buffer provided. No reflection is performed.

simplesect.para: If you are planning to use a CRC based on poly 0x1012 the functions crc16_itu_t() is faster and thus recommended over this one.

parameterdescription.para: The polynomial to use omitting the leading x^16 coefficient
Initial value for the CRC computation
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

simplesect.para: The computed CRC16 value (without any XOR applied to it)

memberdef.argsstring: (uint16_t poly, uint16_t seed, const uint8_t *src, size_t len)
memberdef.name: crc16_reflect

briefdescription.para: Generic function for computing a CRC-16 with input and output reflection.

detaileddescription.para: Compute CRC-16 by passing in the address of the input, the input length and polynomial used in addition to the initial value. This is O(n*8) where n is the length of the buffer provided. Both input and output are reflected.

If you are planning to use a CRC based on poly 0x1012 the function crc16_ccitt() is faster and thus recommended over this one.
The following checksums can, among others, be calculated by this function, depending on the value provided for the initial seed and the value the final calculated CRC is XORed with:

listitem.para: CRC-16/ANSI, CRC-16/MODBUS, CRC-16/USB, CRC-16/IBM https://reveng.sourceforge.io/crc-catalogue/16.htm#crc.cat.crc-16-modbus poly: 0x8005 (0xA001) initial seed: 0xffff, xor output: 0x0000

parameterdescription.para: The polynomial to use omitting the leading x^16 coefficient. Important: please reflect the poly. For example, use 0xA001 instead of 0x8005 for CRC-16-MODBUS.
Initial value for the CRC computation
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

simplesect.para: The computed CRC16 value (without any XOR applied to it)

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t *src, size_t len, uint8_t polynomial, uint8_t initial_value, bool reversed)
memberdef.name: crc8

detaileddescription.para: Compute CRC 8 by passing in the address of the input, the input length and polynomial used in addition to the initial value.

parameterdescription.para: Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes
The polynomial to use omitting the leading x^8 coefficient
Initial value for the CRC computation
Should we use reflected/reversed values or not

briefdescription.para: Compute the checksum of a buffer with polynomial 0x1021, reflecting input and output.

detaileddescription.para: This function is able to calculate any CRC that uses 0x1021 as it polynomial and requires reflecting both the input and the output. It is a fast variant that runs in O(n) time, where n is the length of the input buffer.
The following checksums can, among others, be calculated by this function, depending on the value provided for the initial seed and the value the final calculated CRC is XORed with:

listitem.para: CRC-16/CCITT, CRC-16/CCITT-TRUE, CRC-16/KERMIT https://reveng.sourceforge.io/crc-catalogue/16.htm#crc.cat.crc-16-kermit initial seed: 0x0000, xor output: 0x0000
CRC-16/X-25, CRC-16/IBM-SDLC, CRC-16/ISO-HDLC https://reveng.sourceforge.io/crc-catalogue/16.htm#crc.cat.crc-16-ibm-sdlc initial seed: 0xffff, xor output: 0xffff

detaileddescription.para:
To calculate the CRC across non-contiguous blocks use the return value from block N-1 as the seed for block N.
See ITU-T Recommendation V.41 (November 1988).

parameterdescription.para: Value to seed the CRC with
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

simplesect.para: The computed CRC16 value (without any XOR applied to it)

briefdescription.para: Compute the checksum of a buffer with polynomial 0x1021, no reflection of input or output.

detaileddescription.para: This function is able to calculate any CRC that uses 0x1021 as it polynomial and requires no reflection on both the input and the output. It is a fast variant that runs in O(n) time, where n is the length of the input buffer.
The following checksums can, among others, be calculated by this function, depending on the value provided for the initial seed and the value the final calculated CRC is XORed with:

listitem.para: CRC-16/XMODEM, CRC-16/ACORN, CRC-16/LTE https://reveng.sourceforge.io/crc-catalogue/16.htm#crc.cat.crc-16-xmodem initial seed: 0x0000, xor output: 0x0000
CRC16/CCITT-FALSE, CRC-16/IBM-3740, CRC-16/AUTOSAR https://reveng.sourceforge.io/crc-catalogue/16.htm#crc.cat.crc-16-ibm-3740 initial seed: 0xffff, xor output: 0x0000

detaileddescription.para:
To calculate the CRC across non-contiguous blocks use the return value from block N-1 as the seed for block N.
See ITU-T Recommendation V.41 (November 1988) (MSB first).

parameterdescription.para: Value to seed the CRC with
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

simplesect.para: The computed CRC16 value (without any XOR applied to it)

briefdescription.para: Compute the ANSI (or Modbus) variant of CRC-16.

detaileddescription.para: The ANSI variant of CRC-16 uses 0x8005 (0xA001 reflected) as its polynomial with the initial * value set to 0xffff.

parameterdescription.para: Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes
Pointer to data on which the CRC should be calculated.
Data length.

briefdescription.para: Update an IEEE conforming CRC32 checksum.

parameterdescription.para: CRC32 checksum that needs to be updated.
Pointer to data on which the CRC should be calculated.
Data length.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint32_t crc, const uint8_t *data, size_t len, bool first_pkt, bool last_pkt)
memberdef.name: crc32_c

parameterdescription.para: CRC32C checksum that needs to be updated.
Pointer to data on which the CRC should be calculated.
Data length.
Whether this is the first packet in the stream.
Whether this is the last packet in the stream.

detaileddescription.para: This is a good HD=4 checksum up to 2,147,483,615 bits and HD=5/6 up to 6,167 bits.
Hamming Distance and properties:

listitem.para: initial value (xor-in): provided by caller as crc argument (0xFFFFFFFF is OK)

detaileddescription.para: Reference: https://users.ece.cmu.edu/~koopman/crc/crc32.html

parameterdescription.para: CRC32 checksum that needs to be updated.
Pointer to data on which the CRC should be calculated.
Data length.

detaileddescription.para: Normal CCITT variant of CRC 8 is using 0x07.

parameterdescription.para: Initial value for the CRC computation
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

detaileddescription.para: ROHC (Robust Header Compression) variant of CRC 8. Uses 0x07 as the polynomial with reflection.

parameterdescription.para: Initial value for the CRC computation
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

briefdescription.para: Compute the CRC-7 checksum of a buffer.

detaileddescription.para: See JESD84-A441. Used by the MMC protocol. Uses 0x09 as the polynomial with no reflection. The CRC is left justified, so bit 7 of the result is bit 6 of the CRC.

parameterdescription.para: Value to seed the CRC with
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

briefdescription.para: Compute the CRC-4 checksum of a buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Used by the TMAG5170 sensor. Uses 0x03 as the polynomial with no reflection. 4 most significant bits of the CRC result will be set to zero.

parameterdescription.para: Value to seed the CRC with
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint8_t *src, size_t len, uint8_t polynomial, uint8_t initial_value, bool reversed)
memberdef.name: crc4

detaileddescription.para: Compute CRC 4 by passing in the address of the input, the input length and polynomial used in addition to the initial value. The input buffer must be aligned to a whole byte. It is guaranteed that 4 most significant bits of the result will be set to zero.

parameterdescription.para: Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes
The polynomial to use omitting the leading x^4 coefficient
Initial value for the CRC computation
Should we use reflected/reversed values or not

briefdescription.para: Generate an OpenPGP CRC-24 checksum as defined in RFC 4880 section 6.1.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the data on which the CRC will be calculated.
Data length in bytes.
The CRC-24 checksum that needs to be updated. The full 32-bit value of the CRC needs to be used between calls, do not mask the value to keep only the last 24 bits.
A pointer to the data on which the CRC will be calculated.
Data length in bytes.

simplesect.para: The CRC-24 value. When the last buffer of data has been processed, mask the value with CRC24_FINAL_VALUE_MASK to keep only the meaningful 24 bits of the CRC result.

briefdescription.para: Calculate an RTCM3 CRC24Q frame checksum.

parameterdescription.para: RTCM3 Frame
Frame length in bytes.

simplesect.para: 0 if the data-frame contains a checksum and it matches.
Result if data-frame does not contain checksum.

memberdef.argsstring: (enum crc_type type, const uint8_t *src, size_t len, uint32_t seed, uint32_t poly, bool reflect, bool first, bool last)
memberdef.name: crc_by_type

briefdescription.para: Compute a CRC checksum, in a generic way.

detaileddescription.para: This is a dispatch function that calls the individual CRC routine determined by type.
For 7, 8, 16 and 24-bit CRCs, the relevant seed and poly values should be passed in via the least-significant byte(s).
Similarly, for 7, 8, 16 and 24-bit CRCs, the relevant result is stored in the least-significant byte(s) of the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: CRC algorithm to use.
Input bytes for the computation
Length of the input in bytes
Seed or existing CRC value to update
The polynomial to use omitting the leading coefficient
Should we use reflected/reversed values or not
Whether this is the first packet in the stream.
Whether this is the last packet in the stream.
